entities.name: ENZYME-LINKED IMMUNOSORBENT ASSAY (ELISA)
entities.description: A widely employed serological test used for detecting various antigens

entities.name: DOUBLE ANTIBODY SANDWICH ELISA (DAS-ELISA)
entities.description: An ELISA technique used for the detection of rhizobia and assessment of inoculum quality

entities.name: DIRECT ANTIGEN COATING ELISA (DAC-ELISA)
entities.description: A simple ELISA tool used for virus detection in field surveys and seed samples

entities.name: PROTEIN A COATING ELISA (PAC-ELISA)
entities.description: An ELISA procedure used for the detection and assay of viruses

entities.name: COMPETITIVE ELISA
entities.description: An ELISA procedure developed to detect aflatoxin B in seed samples

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM
entities.description: Soil bacteria used for detection and quality assessment, including the ability to distinguish between different strains

entities.name: VIRUSES
entities.description: Pathogenic agents detected in crops using various ELISA techniques

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: An agricultural crop used for virus and aflatoxin detection studies

entities.name: AFLATOXIN B
entities.description: A mycotoxin detected in groundnut seed samples

entities.name: INOCULUM
entities.description: The material used for inoculation, whose quality is assessed via DAS-ELISA

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium where the establishment of inoculated rhizobial strains is assessed

relationships.description: used to assess establishment of strains in
